name: Marcus Thompson
chief_complaint: I've got this sharp pain right in the middle of my chest that gets worse when I breathe in or lean back, and I had a cold about a week ago
onset_description: Started yesterday afternoon with mild chest discomfort that he initially thought was muscle soreness from the gym. Woke up this morning and the pain was much worse, especially with deep breaths. Got scared it might be a heart attack and came to the ED.

chest_pain.description: Sharp, stabbing pain right in the center of my chest, gets way worse when I take a deep breath or lean back
chest_pain.onset: yesterday afternoon, significantly worse this morning
chest_pain.radiation: to the left side of the neck and left trapezius area
associated_symptoms: mild shortness of breath from guarding

shortness_of_breath.description: Not really short of breath, more like I'm afraid to take a deep breath because it hurts so bad
shortness_of_breath.character: splinting due to pain rather than true dyspnea

low_grade_fever.description: Been feeling warm and achy since last night, kind of like the tail end of being sick

malaise.description: Just feeling run down, like I'm not over this cold yet

labs.CBC: WBC 11.2 (mildly elevated), Hgb 14.8, Plt 280 - mild leukocytosis
labs.BMP: Na 140, K 4.3, Cr 0.9, Glucose 92 - BUN 14 - all within normal limits
labs.ECG: Diffuse ST elevation with PR depression in leads II, III, aVF, and V3-V6. No reciprocal ST depression. Consistent with acute pericarditis.

past_medical_history: Recent viral upper respiratory infection (resolved 5 days ago)
medications: Cetirizine 10mg daily as needed for allergies

social_history.alcohol: Social drinker, maybe 2-3 drinks on weekends with friends
social_history.drugs: Occasional marijuana, maybe once or twice a month
social_history.living_situation: Lives with two roommates in an apartment near campus
social_history.exercise: Very active - lifts weights 4 times a week, runs 3 times a week
social_history.diet: Tries to eat high protein for muscle building, tracks macros, eats fairly healthy

conditions.maternal_grandfather: Died of colon cancer at age 72

personality: anxious young man who is health-conscious and frightened this might be a heart attack at his age, otherwise friendly and cooperative
communication_style: Polite and engaged. Will ask a lot of questions driven by anxiety ('Could this be a heart attack? I'm only 28'). Has done some Googling of his symptoms and may reference things he read online. Responds well to reassurance with clear explanations. Open and honest about all habits including marijuana use.
pain_description_style: Descriptive and specific ('it's like someone stabbing me when I breathe in'). Can clearly identify what makes it better and worse. Will demonstrate positions that help (leaning forward). Distinguishes well between pain at rest versus with breathing.

critical_findings: Classic pericarditis presentation: sharp pleuritic chest pain relieved by leaning forward
Pain radiates to left trapezius (pathognomonic for pericarditis)
Recent viral URI as likely etiology (post-viral pericarditis)
ECG showing diffuse ST elevation with PR depression (classic pericarditis pattern without reciprocal changes)
Low-grade fever consistent with inflammatory/infectious etiology
Normal D-dimer helps rule out pulmonary embolism
Young patient with minimal cardiac risk factors - important to differentiate from ACS
differential_diagnoses: Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI - must differentiate ECG pattern)
recommended_interventions: Ibuprofen 600mg TID (first-line NSAID for pericarditis) with GI protection
Echocardiogram to rule out pericardial effusion and assess cardiac function
Serial troponins to ensure trending down
Avoid strenuous exercise until symptoms resolve and inflammatory markers normalize
Return precautions: worsening chest pain, syncope, worsening shortness of breath
